question_text: How many coin flips are needed to determine if a coin lands heads 75% of the time versus the expected 50%, with 80% power at alpha=0.05 (one-sided)?
r_code: pwr.p.test(h = ES.h(p1 = 0.75, p2 = 0.50), sig.level = 0.05, power = 0.80, alternative = "greater")

question_text: What is the power to detect a 75% heads rate versus 50% with 40 coin flips at alpha=0.01 (one-sided)?
r_code: pwr.p.test(h = ES.h(p1 = 0.75, p2 = 0.50), sig.level = 0.01, n = 40, alternative = "greater")

question_text: What is the power to detect 75% heads versus 50% with 40 flips at alpha=0.01 (two-sided)?
How many coin flips are needed to detect a 65% heads rate versus 50% with 80% power at alpha=0.05 (two-sided)?
What is the power with n=20, alpha=0.05 for small (h=0.2), medium (h=0.5), and large (h=0.8) effect sizes?
How many subjects per group are needed to detect a difference between 55% and 50% alcohol preference between genders at 80% power and alpha=0.05?
How many subjects per group to detect a difference between proportions of 10% and 5% with 80% power and alpha=0.05?
What is the power with 543 males and 675 females surveyed, small effect size h=0.2, alpha=0.05?
With 763 females surveyed, how many males are needed to detect h=0.2 with 80% power at alpha=0.05?
What is the power of a two-sample t-test with n=30 per group, medium effect d=0.5, alpha=0.05 (two-sided)?
How many subjects per group to detect medium effect d=0.5 with 80% power at alpha=0.05 (two-sided)?
How many subjects per group to detect a 75-cent difference in coffee prices (SD=$2.25, so d=0.333) with 80% power at alpha=0.05?
How many transactions needed to detect a $0.50 difference from $3.00 average coffee purchase (SD=$2.25, d=0.222) with 90% power at alpha=0.05 (one-sided, greater)?
What is the power to detect a 0.08-second improvement in 40-yard dash (SD of differences=0.25, d=0.32) with 24 pairs at alpha=0.05 (one-sided)?
How many pairs needed to detect a 0.08-second improvement (SD differences=0.25, d=0.32) with 80% power at alpha=0.05 (one-sided)?
How many pairs needed if within-pair SD is 0.1 (not SD of differences=0.25), giving d=0.08/(0.1*sqrt(2))=0.566, with 80% power?
What is the power with n1=28, n2=35, d=0.5, alpha=0.05?
What is the power to detect unequal preference among 4 package designs (3/8 vs 5/24 each) with N=100, df=3, alpha=0.05?
r_code: null <- rep(0.25, 4); alt <- c(3/8, rep((5/8)/3, 3)); pwr.chisq.test(w = ES.w1(null, alt), N = 100, df = 3, sig.level = 0.05)

question_text: How many subjects needed to detect the same design preference pattern with 80% power at alpha=0.05?
r_code: pwr.chisq.test(w = ES.w1(null, alt), df = 3, power = 0.8, sig.level = 0.05)

question_text: What is the power to detect a gender-flossing association with N=100, df=1, alpha=0.01?
r_code: prob <- matrix(c(0.1, 0.2, 0.4, 0.3), ncol = 2); pwr.chisq.test(w = ES.w2(prob), N = 100, df = 1, sig.level = 0.01)

question_text: How many subjects needed to detect the gender-flossing association with 90% power at alpha=0.01?
How many subjects needed to detect a small effect (w=0.1) with 90% power at alpha=0.01 and df=1?
How many subjects needed to detect r=0.1 (small) with 80% power at alpha=0.01 (one-sided, greater)?
How many subjects needed to detect r=0.1 with 80% power at alpha=0.01 (two-sided)?
How many subjects per group needed to compare 3 fuel types on diesel car MPG with medium effect f=0.25, 90% power at alpha=0.01?
How many subjects per group with k=3, between-group SD=5, within-group SD=3 (f=5/3=1.67), 90% power at alpha=0.01?
How many student records needed to predict first-year GPA from SAT and class rank (u=2 predictors, R-squared=0.30, f2=0.4286) with 80% power at alpha=0.001?
What is the power of the GPA regression model with 40 subjects, 2 predictors, R-squared=0.30, at alpha=0.01?
How many subjects per group for a balanced one-way ANOVA with 4 groups, medium effect f=0.25, 80% power at alpha=0.05?
What is the power of a one-tailed two-sample t-test with n=12 per group and d=0.75 at alpha=0.05?
What effect size h can be detected with n=20 per group, alpha=0.05, and 75% power?

A clinical dietician compares two diets (A and B) for diabetic patients. Diet A is expected to yield 10 mg/dl lower blood glucose than Diet B. SD for Diet A is 15, Diet B is 17. How many subjects per group for 80% power at alpha=0.05?
Same diet comparison: what is the power with 30 subjects per group (equal allocation)?
Same diet comparison with 60 total subjects split unequally (40 and 20). What is the power?
Compare 4 teaching methods for math standardized test scores (means: 550, 598, 598, 646; SD=80). How many students per group for 80% power at alpha=0.05?
r_code: groupmeans <- c(550, 598, 598, 646); power.anova.test(groups = length(groupmeans), between.var = var(groupmeans), within.var = 6400, power = 0.823, sig.level = 0.05)

question_text: Same teaching study: what is the power with 25 subjects per group?
r_code: power.anova.test(groups = 4, between.var = var(c(550,598,598,646)), within.var = 6400, n = 25, sig.level = 0.05)

question_text: Compare 4 teaching methods with medium effect (means: 550, 580, 580, 610; SD=80). How many students per group for 80% power?
r_code: groupmeans <- c(550,580,580,610); power.anova.test(groups = 4, between.var = var(groupmeans), within.var = 6400, power = 0.8, sig.level = 0.05)

question_text: Compare 4 teaching methods with small effect (means: 550, 560, 560, 570; SD=80). How many students per group for 80% power?
r_code: groupmeans <- c(550,560,560,570); power.anova.test(groups = 4, between.var = var(groupmeans), within.var = 6400, power = 0.8, sig.level = 0.05)

question_text: Test the effect of mother's education on English language proficiency (R-squared increase=0.03, residual variance=0.52, 5 total predictors, testing 1 predictor). How many subjects for 80% power at alpha=0.05?
Test language spoken at home (2 dummy variables) on English proficiency (R-squared increase=0.025, 5 total predictors, testing 2 df). How many for 80% power at alpha=0.05?
A manufacturer claims light bulbs last 850 hours (SD=50). Consumer advocates suspect actual lifespan is 40 hours less (810 hours). How many bulbs to test for 90% power at alpha=0.05 (two-tailed)?
Same light bulb scenario, but only 10 bulbs available. What is the power?
Test whether a weight loss program achieves the claimed 5-pound loss (SD of differences=5, two-tailed). How many participants for 80% power at alpha=0.05?
Compare dominant vs non-dominant hand dexterity (mean difference=5 sec, SD=10). What is the power with 35 subjects at alpha=0.05 (two-tailed)?
Same dexterity study with one-tailed test. What is the power?
Weight loss with pre-SD=7, post-SD=12, correlation=0.5, mean difference=5 lbs. How many for 80% power at alpha=0.05?
Drug A vs placebo study (Drug A mean=5, SD=2.5; placebo mean=3, SD=2.5). How many subjects for 91% power at alpha=0.05?
Drug A vs placebo (conservative: Drug A mean=4.5, SD=3; placebo mean=3.5, SD=3). How many for 90% power at alpha=0.05?
How many subjects needed to detect a correlation of r=0.6 with 80% power at alpha=0.05?
Drug A improves health in 60% of patients vs 30% in placebo group. How many for 80% power at alpha=0.05?
A drug reduces HbA1c in type 2 diabetes (Cohen's d=0.66). How many per arm for 80% power at alpha=0.05 (two-sided)?
r_code: pwr::pwr.t.test(n = NULL, sig.level = 0.05, type = "two.sample", alternative = "two.sided", power = 0.80, d = 0.66)

question_text: Pilot study of enema treatment to lower serum creatinine by >=1.0 g/dL (SD=2, d=0.5). How many participants for 80% power at alpha=0.05 (one-sided)?
r_code: pwr::pwr.t.test(n = NULL, sig.level = 0.05, type = "one.sample", alternative = "greater", power = 0.8, d = 0.5)

question_text: Paired design with large effect d=0.8. How many paired subjects for 80% power at alpha=0.05 (two-sided)?

result.note: vs 26 per arm for two-sample design

r_code: pwr::pwr.t.test(n = NULL, sig.level = 0.05, type = "paired", alternative = "two.sided", power = 0.8, d = 0.8)

question_text: Systolic BP reduction study (d=0.5) with n1=40 fixed. How many subjects in the second arm for 80% power at alpha=0.05?
r_code: pwr::pwr.t2n.test(n1 = 40, n2 = NULL, sig.level = 0.05, alternative = "two.sided", power = 0.8, d = 0.5)

question_text: How many participants to detect r=0.4 with 80% power at alpha=0.05 (two-sided)?
How many participants to detect r=0.3 with 80% power at alpha=0.01 (two-sided)?
What is the power with n=215, observed r=0.48, alpha=0.01?
New York Scholarship Program: treatment (n=291) vs control (n=230), d=0.257, alpha=0.05. What is the power?
Post-achievement regressed on voucher status (R-squared=0.016, 1 predictor, v=519 residual df). What is the power at alpha=0.05?
Post-achievement with voucher and pre-achievement (R-squared=0.442, 2 predictors, v=518). What is the power at alpha=0.05?
Determine sample size for a nitrogen level experiment with 6 groups, medium effect f=0.25, 80% power at alpha=0.05.
Compute power for a fitted nitrogen-yield model with k=6 groups, n=571 per group, f=0.1343 at alpha=0.05.
r_code: EtaSQ <- 23987/(23987+1330110); f <- sqrt(EtaSQ/(1-EtaSQ)); pwr.anova.test(k=6, n=571, f=f, sig.level=0.05)

question_text: Sample size for a factorial ANOVA interaction with 2 numerator df, medium f2=0.25, 80% power at alpha=0.05.
Within-subject design with 5 subjects and 3 treatments (means: 20, 20.5, 21; SD=1). What is the power at alpha=0.05?
r_code: mod1 <- lmer(Y ~ treatment + (1|subject), data=data); powerSim(mod1, alpha=0.05)

question_text: Greenhouse study with 4 treatments, n=6 per group, max difference=8.2 between means, SD=1.747. What is the power?
Greenhouse with 3 treatments only (means: 28.6, 25.87, 29.2; SD=1.933), n=6 per group. What is the power?
Greenhouse with 4 treatments (means: 21, 28.6, 25.87, 29.2; SD=1.747). How many replicates per group for 80% power?
Intervention study: treatment vs control on depressive symptoms (d=0.5, alpha=0.10). What is the power with n=30 per group?
r_code: set.seed(1); group1 <- rnorm(30, 1, 2); group2 <- rnorm(30, 0, 2); t.test(group1, group2, paired = FALSE, var.equal = TRUE, conf.level = 0.90)

question_text: Pre-post intervention (d=0.5, mean difference=1, SD=2, alpha=0.10). How many participants for 95% power?
r_code: set.seed(1); difference <- rnorm(n, 1, 2); t.test(difference, mu = 0, conf.level = 0.90)$p.value

question_text: What is the power to detect SMD=0.2 in a meta-analysis of 10 studies, each with n1=n2=25, at alpha=0.05?
Same meta-analysis (SMD=0.2, K=10, n1=n2=25) but with moderate heterogeneity. What is the power?
r_code: power.analysis(d = 0.2, k = 10, n1 = 25, n2 = 25, p = 0.05, heterogeneity = "moderate")

question_text: Two subgroups with SMD=0.30 (SE=0.13) and SMD=0.66 (SE=0.14). What is the power to detect the difference?

source_type: SLCLADAL Power Analysis in R tutorial (simr)
question_text: Group x SentenceType interaction in LMER with 20 participants and 24 sentences. What is the power?
r_code: sim_gst <- simr::powerSim(m1, nsim=20, test=fcompare(y ~ WordOrder + Group + SentenceType))

source_type: SLCLADAL Power Analysis in R tutorial (simr)
question_text: Same LMER but extended to 120 sentences. What is the power for the interaction?
r_code: m1_as <- simr::extend(m1, along="Sentence", n=120); powerSim(m1_as, nsim=20, test=fcompare(y ~ WordOrder + Group + SentenceType))

source_type: SLCLADAL Power Analysis in R tutorial (simr)
question_text: Same LMER extended to 120 participants. What is the power for the interaction?
r_code: m1_ap <- simr::extend(m1, along="ID", n=120); powerSim(m1_ap, nsim=20, test=fcompare(y ~ WordOrder + Group + SentenceType))

How many observations to detect r=0.3 with 80% power at alpha=0.05?
How many observations to detect r=0.4 with 80% power at alpha=0.05?
Two binary predictors with interaction. P(y=1) varies from 0.30 to 0.85 across cells. What is the power with n=200 at alpha=0.05?
r_code: n <- 200; p_out <- replicate(n = 1000, expr = { x <- sample(0:1, size = n, replace = TRUE); z <- sample(0:1, size = n, replace = TRUE); p <- plogis(b0 + b1*(x==1) + b2*(z==1) + b3*(x==1)*(z==1)); y <- rbinom(n=n, size=1, prob=p); d <- data.frame(y, x, z); m <- glm(y ~ x + z + x:z, data=d, family=binomial); summary(m)$coefficients[4,4] < 0.05 }); mean(p_out)

question_text: Same logistic regression interaction: what sample size needed for 80% power at alpha=0.05?
Same interaction with alpha=0.01. What are the powers at n=350-500?
Treatment x time interaction in nested design (5 classes, 10 students, 3 timepoints). Fixed effects: treat:time1=1, treat:time2=0.9. Power?
Same design but treat:time1 interaction increased from 1 to 2. Power?
Extended to 20 classes (from 5), 10 students, 3 timepoints. Power?
5 classes, 20 students per class (doubled from 10), 3 timepoints. Power?
LMM with fixed intercept=2, slope=-0.1, random intercept variance=0.5, residual SD=1. Power for slope with 30 obs and 3 groups?
r_code: model1 <- makeLmer(y ~ x + (1|g), fixef=c(2, -0.1), VarCorr=0.5, sigma=1, data=X); powerSim(model1, nsim=20)

question_text: Poisson GLMM with random slope and intercept. Fixed intercept=2, slope=-0.1. Power for slope with 30 obs and 3 groups?
r_code: model2 <- makeGlmer(z ~ x + (x|g), family="poisson", fixef=c(2, -0.1), VarCorr=matrix(c(0.5, 0.05, 0.05, 0.1), 2), data=X); powerSim(model2, nsim=20)
